findings.message: 'comprehensive' is a vague quantifier in the plugin description; does not specify what coverage means in practice
CLAUDE.md project structure tree lists 2 commands (setup.md, check-usage.md) but 4 command files exist: setup.md, check-usage.md, setup-alias.md, update.md
$ARGUMENTS is passed unquoted to the node shell invocation; user-supplied shell metacharacters (semicolons, backticks, dollar signs) execute as arbitrary shell commands
Step-2 heading 'add the appropriate function' uses vague quantifier 'appropriate'; selection criteria are specified in sub-steps but the heading itself is imprecise
Command appends a shell function to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc (or PowerShell $PROFILE) outside the plugin repository
Bash(cat:*) declared in allowed-tools but no task step in the command body uses cat; file creation uses Write and jq instead
Command writes ~/.claude/settings.json via jq and mv, modifying a file outside the plugin repository
Command writes ~/.claude/settings.json via jq and mv to update the statusLine path after a plugin version bump
All four devDependencies use ^ version ranges: @types/node ^20.0.0, esbuild ^0.20.0, typescript ^5.0.0, vitest ^4.0.16; minor or patch bumps may introduce breaking changes in the build pipeline
'comprehensive' is a vague quantifier in the plugin description; does not specify what coverage means in practice
Step-2 heading 'add the appropriate function' uses vague quantifier 'appropriate'; selection criteria are specified in sub-steps but the heading itself is imprecise
Bash(cat:*) declared in allowed-tools but no task step in the command body uses cat; file creation uses Write and jq instead

Command writes ~/.claude/settings.json via jq and mv to update the statusLine path after a plugin version bump
